summary: discovery-build postinstall exfiltrates host/CI identity to attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator domain
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js runs automatically and collects installer-side identity data — `whoami` (via execSync), `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, current working directory, and CI-related environment variables including `CI`, `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, and `NODE_ENV` — then beacons the data via HTTPS GET (and DNS lookup) to `kf3xr2idaeyvj255ohkhcal9g0msasyh.oastify.com`, a Burp Collaborator subdomain controlled by whoever published the package. The hardcoded callback host is defined at postinstall.js:5 as `CALLBACK_HOST` and used at L21-23 in `https.get({ host: CALLBACK_HOST, path,... })`. The package self-describes as a 'security research canary,' but installers do not consent to outbound beacons of their CI repository identity and host metadata, and the captured fields (GITHUB_REPOSITORY in particular) reveal which private repos and build environments installed the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
